title: digitaria wallichiana
intro: Digitaria wallichiana, also known as Dhaman grass, is a type of grass species native to the Indian subcontinent. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Digitaria wallichiana include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive disorders, as well as its use as an anti-diarrheal and anti-emetic agent. In herbal preparations, it is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, and powders, which can be administered orally or topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
